{"problem_standardized_math":"Consider the optimization problem\n\\[\n\\begin{aligned}\n&\\min_{x,y} f(x,y) \\stackrel{\\mathrm{def}}{=} \\frac{1}{2}(x-2)^2+\\frac{1}{2}\\left(y-\\frac{1}{2}\\right)^2 \\\\\n\\text{subject to}\\quad &(x+1)^{-1}-y-\\frac{1}{4}\\ge 0,\\\\\n&x\\ge 0,\\\\\n&y\\ge 0.\n\\end{aligned}\n\\tag{15.5}\n\\]\nLabel the three constraints, in order, by the indices 1, 2, and 3. Show that none of the working sets \\(\\mathcal{W}=\\{3\\}\\), \\(\\mathcal{W}=\\{1,2\\}\\), and \\(\\mathcal{W}=\\{2,3\\}\\) is the optimal active set for problem \\((15.5)\\)."}
